rulings.Case Title: JOHN T BALL VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 and 2 (general negligence excluding negligent failure to warn, strict liability design and manufacturing defect), denied as to issues 3 and 4 (negligent failure to warn and strict liability failure to warn), taken off calendar as to issue 5 (false representation) and denied as to issue 6 (punitive damages). As to issues 1 and 2, defendant satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. As to issues 3 and 4, defendant initially shifted its burden but plaintiffs produced evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant¿s duty to warn was discharged under the circumstances of this case to deliver an effective warning to decedent, a non-bystander. As to issue 6, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs have dismissed their false representation cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID BROCKMAN, AS PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OF THE et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Autozone West, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs¿ claims against it are time barred and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue. The allegations included in the May 19, 2011 complaint demonstrate that at the time plaintiffs filed such complaint they were not genuinely ignorant of defendant¿s true name or the facts rendering it liable. (See Complaint for Survival, Wrongful Death, 20:5-11; defendant¿s material fact no. 9.) While the original complaint was timely pursuant to C.C.P. 340.2 the relation back doctrine does not apply and the amendment which substituted defendant in for a Doe defendant was untimely. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 3 and 4 (negligence, premises/contractor liability and punitive damages) and off calendar as to issue 2 (strict liability). As to summary judgment and summary adjudication issues 1 and 3, defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony, declaration and defendant¿s General Order 129 interrogatory responses create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to its responses to General Order interrogatories are overruled. The Court did not consider Mr. Ay¿s declaration for purposes of this motion. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff represents that he has dismissed his strict liability cause of action, although there is not yet a dismissal on file. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN CHOW et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 11, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Specially appearing defendant Victory Real Estate Development Corporation¿s motion to quash service of summons for lack of personal jurisdiction is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)
